type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: According to Ontario climate data on Environment Canada's National Climate Data and Information Archive, 2012 had much higher average temperatures through January, February and March compared to the same periods in 2011. With the exception of periodic emergency generator testing, Part 4 substances reported for the University of Toronto are created through natural gas combustion of comfort heating equipment. The 2012 emissions are lower than 2011 by >10% because less natural gas was required during these peak winter months.

reasonsForNoActivities.fr: Autres (précisez dans le champ des commentaires)

p2Plan.noActivitiesComment: Specific activities have not been undertaken in 2022, however, it is the University's long term plan to reduce dependency on and use of fossil fuels. The primary source of CACs at the University of Toronto are the Central Steam Plant Boilers. There are plans in the works to replace one or more of these natural gas boilers over the next few years with electric boilers. Ground level heat pumps are also being explored as an option for reducing fossil fuel requirements on campus.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Natural gas is consumed for comfort heating. The volume of natural gas consumed each year depending on occupancy demands of UofT buildings. This demand is based on the frequency and intensity of cold days and can vary significantly from year to year.
